content.body: Every day in pharmacy practice, you will make decisions that affect people's health, privacy, and wellbeing. Ethics provides the framework for making these decisions correctly — not just legally, but morally. While the law sets the minimum standard of behaviour, professional ethics calls you to a higher standard. The difference between a competent pharmacy technician and an excellent one often comes down to ethical judgement.

content.text: Pharmacy technicians are guided by ethical principles that prioritize patient safety, confidentiality, integrity, professional competence, and collaboration within healthcare teams.

content.body: Patient Safety First: A pharmacy technician’s primary responsibility is to ensure the health and safety of patients by using their knowledge and skills effectively in all aspects of care.Integrity and Honesty: Technicians must uphold honesty and integrity in all professional actions, comply with laws, and maintain high moral and ethical standards. This includes reporting any illegal or unethical conduct without fear or favor.Confidentiality: Protecting patient information is essential. Pharmacy technicians must respect patient privacy, maintain confidentiality of medical records, and only disclose information with proper authorization.Professional Competency: Continuous learning and skill enhancement are required to maintain professional competence and provide accurate information about medications and healthcare services.Respect for Patients and Colleagues: Technicians must respect the individuality, dignity, and rights of patients, as well as value the abilities of pharmacists, colleagues, and other healthcare professionals to foster a collaborative environment.Quality Assurance: Technicians should never assist in dispensing, promoting, or distributing medications or medical devices that do not meet legal or quality standards.Professional Conduct: Pharmacy technicians must avoid actions that discredit the profession and actively support organizations that promote the pharmacy field.Equity and Non-Discrimination: Ethical practice requires treating all individuals equally, regardless of lifestyle, religion, race, gender, age, or health status, and ensuring informed choices about healthcare.Teamwork and Collaboration: Working effectively within a multidisciplinary healthcare team is essential to optimize patient care and ensure safe, efficient service delivery.

Ethical principles for pharmacy technicians provide a framework for professional behavior, guiding interactions with patients, colleagues, and the broader healthcare system. By adhering to these principles, technicians maintain public trust, ensure patient safety, and contribute to the integrity and effectiveness of pharmacy practice

When facing an ethical dilemma, ask yourself: 'Would I be comfortable if my decision was published in tomorrow's newspaper? Would I be comfortable explaining this decision to the Pharmacy Board?' If the answer to either question is 'no', reconsider your course of action.
content.title: The Ethical Litmus Test
